title: VIP Interneurons in Cortical Computation: From Molecular Identity to Circuit Function
thesis_question: How do VIP-expressing GABAergic interneurons — defined by CGE lineage and a distinctive transcriptomic, morphological, and physiological profile — implement disinhibitory and gain-modulatory operations that shape cortical computation, and to what extent does the canonical 'VIP disinhibitor' framing survive scrutiny under contemporary multi-modal evidence?
audience: Systems and circuits neuroscientists, computational neuroscientists, and graduate-level researchers working on cortical interneurons, inhibitory microcircuits, and behaviorally relevant cortical dynamics; assumes familiarity with cortical anatomy, basic electrophysiology, and standard mouse-genetics tools.

evidence_parameters.saturation_criterion: <2% new unique in last 100 records, confirmed across two consecutive database passes

sections.title: Introduction
sections.scope_notes: Frames the problem: why VIP interneurons matter for cortical computation; historical arc from Bayraktar/Rudy era markers to contemporary transcriptomic and disinhibition frameworks; introduces the canonical disinhibitor model and signposts the reassessment in Section 13.

sections.title: Molecular Identity and Transcriptomic Taxonomy
sections.scope_notes: Defines VIP interneurons at the molecular level: VIP/ChAT/CR/CCK co-expression, Tasic/Allen and BICCN taxonomies, Patch-seq integration, comparison with SST and PV reference taxonomies, cross-species conservation of VIP transcriptomic types.
key_topics: VIP subtypes (bipolar vs multipolar transcriptomic correlates)

sections.title: Developmental Origins and Postnatal Maturation
sections.scope_notes: Caudal ganglionic eminence specification, key transcription factors (Prox1, COUP-TFII, Sp8, Nr2f2), migratory routes, postnatal integration, activity-dependent maturation, critical periods.

sections.title: Morphological Diversity
sections.scope_notes: Bipolar, bitufted, multipolar, small basket, and arcade morphologies; layer-specific dendritic/axonal targeting; reconstructions from MICrONS, Allen, and EM connectomes; morphology-electrophysiology-transcriptome (MET) correspondence.

sections.title: Intrinsic Electrophysiology
sections.scope_notes: Resting properties, AP waveform, firing patterns (irregular spiking, continuous adapting, bursting), sag/Ih, K+ channel repertoire, intrinsic excitability differences across VIP subtypes; comparison to PV fast-spiking and SST low-threshold spiking baselines.

sections.title: Synaptic Properties and Connectivity
sections.scope_notes: Inputs to VIP cells (long-range cortico-cortical, thalamic, neuromodulatory), outputs (preferential SST targeting, weaker PV/Pyr targets), short-term plasticity, GABA receptor composition, paired-recording datasets, EM connectomic counts.

sections.title: Local Circuit Motifs and the Disinhibition Framework
sections.scope_notes: Canonical VIP→SST→Pyr disinhibitory motif; alternative motifs (VIP→PV, VIP→Pyr direct); inter-areal disinhibition; quantitative motif weights from connectivity datasets; how 'disinhibition' is operationalized across studies.

sections.title: In Vivo Function During Behavior
sections.scope_notes: Calcium imaging and electrophysiology of VIP cells during locomotion, attention, reinforcement, reward, learning, and prediction error; optogenetic/chemogenetic perturbation outcomes; cell-type-specific contribution to cortical state changes.

sections.title: VIP Interneurons Across Brain Regions
sections.scope_notes: Region-specific VIP biology: V1, S1, A1, mPFC, retrosplenial, entorhinal, hippocampus (CA1/CA3 VIP types), striatum and amygdala VIP populations; conserved vs region-specific motifs and functions.

sections.title: Oscillatory Dynamics and Temporal Coordination
sections.scope_notes: VIP contribution to gamma, theta, beta, and cross-frequency coupling; phase-locking of VIP firing; modeling of VIP-driven rhythm gating; comparison with PV's canonical gamma role.

sections.title: Species Differences, Human Relevance, and Disease
sections.scope_notes: Mouse-human VIP comparisons (Hodge BICCN, Krienen primate); human-specific transcriptomic types; disease relevance: Rett (Mecp2), autism (Cntnap2, Shank3), schizophrenia, epilepsy, Alzheimer's, depression; therapeutic implications.

sections.title: Computational Models of VIP Circuit Function
sections.scope_notes: Mean-field and spiking models of VIP-SST-PV-Pyr microcircuits; gain modulation; predictive coding implementations; reinforcement-learning roles; circuit-level model fitting to imaging data.

sections.title: Synthesis and Conclusion: Reassessing the Canonical VIP Disinhibitor
sections.scope_notes: Synthesizes evidence across all sections to evaluate whether the canonical 'VIP = disinhibitor' framing is empirically warranted, what alternative framings (gain controller, context gate, neuromodulator relay) better fit the data, and what experiments would adjudicate; outlines open questions and a forward agenda.
key_topics: alternatives: gain control / context gate / NM relay

clusters.name: Developmental Origins and Postnatal Maturation (CGE lineage)
Disinhibition Motif and Local Circuit Architecture
In Vivo VIP Activity and Behavioral Function
VIP Across Brain Regions (Cortical Areas, Hippocampus, Subcortical)
Species Differences, Human Relevance, and Disease
Computational Models of VIP Circuit Function
SST/PV Context for VIP (Adjacent Interneuron Literature)
clusters.expected_envelope: 250-350 papers (filtered to entries co-cited with VIP context)

steps.title: Confirm scope, thesis, and cluster design from user prompt and TOC
steps.description: Read provenance/review_request.md verbatim. Define 13 sections (1:1 with user TOC) and 13 clusters covering molecular taxonomy, CGE development, morphology, intrinsic ephys, synaptic connectivity, disinhibition motif, in vivo function, brain regions, oscillations, species/disease, computational models, SST/PV context, and neuromodulation. Embed evidence_parameters (saturation <2% over 100 records confirmed across 2 consecutive passes; snowball=2 rounds; min_papers_per_cluster=null; bibliography target ≥2,000). Coordinator verifies: scope.json schema valid, sections=13, clusters=13, evidence_parameters present and parsed, review_request.md+.txt captured byte-identically.

steps.title: Run cluster-by-cluster saturation search (PubMed + bioRxiv + Semantic Scholar + OpenAlex)
steps.description: For each of the 13 clusters, search across PubMed, bioRxiv/medRxiv, Semantic Scholar, and OpenAlex using the cluster's search_anchors. Continue until <2% new unique papers in last 100 records, confirmed across two consecutive database passes. Then perform 2 snowball rounds (forward + backward citation chasing) on top-50 most-cited per cluster. Extract findings, methods, sample sizes, key claims; require ≥50% with full-text retrieved (fetch_article_fulltext).

steps.title: Aggregate, dedupe, and emit per-cluster evidence packages
steps.description: Dedupe by DOI/title across clusters; emit per-cluster JSON with paper metadata, extracted findings, snowball provenance, and saturation curves. Coordinator verifies: total unique bibliography ≥2,000; saturation criterion met (confirmed-across-2-passes evidence in saturation logs); snowball_rounds=2 logged per cluster; ≥50% full-text rate per cluster; no cluster blocked by zero results.

steps.title: Build citation_key_map and author_name_table via CrossRef
steps.description: Resolve each unique DOI from Phase 2 against CrossRef; build citation_key_map (canonical author-year-keyword keys, ASCII-only) and author_name_table (canonical name spellings, ORCID where available, disambiguation flags). Coordinator verifies: 100% of Phase 2 DOIs resolved or flagged; zero key collisions; ASCII-only keys; author table covers all first/last authors.

steps.title: Draft argument arc, per-section plans, figure specs, and style guide
steps.description: Produce a top-level argument arc that culminates in the Section 13 reassessment of the canonical disinhibitor framing. For each of the 13 sections, draft a section plan (2-4 subsections, claim outline, target citations, planned figures/notebooks) and a figure spec sheet. Emit style_guide.md (terminology, abbreviations, units, MyST conventions). Coordinator verifies: 13 section plans present; each plan names ≥1 figure or 'no figure' rationale; argument arc traces from Sec1 → Sec13.

steps.title: Assign clusters to sections and emit per-section evidence packages
steps.description: Map each cluster's papers onto target sections per scope.json; build per-section evidence packages containing the relevant subset, scaffold extracts (claim → candidate citations → quoted snippets), and figure_data candidates. Coordinator verifies: every section has ≥1 cluster assigned; orphan papers (no section) flagged; per-section package counts logged; figure_data candidates list ≥1 source per planned figure.

steps.title: Blinded review of figure_data — verdicts PASS/CAVEAT/SPLIT/REDESIGN
steps.description: Parallel critics inspect each planned figure's underlying figure_data for unit consistency, species/age comparability, normalization compatibility, and statistical defensibility. Emit verdict per figure (PASS / CAVEAT / SPLIT-INTO-PANELS / REDESIGN) with rationale. Coordinator verifies: every Phase 4 figure has a verdict; REDESIGN verdicts trigger return to Phase 4 plan update before Phase 7 starts.

steps.title: Draft each body section (parallel writers, one per section) — MyST + LaTeX + figures + notebooks
steps.description: One LITREVIEW writer per body section (sections 2–12, plus Sec 13 synthesis). Each writer produces MyST source, LaTeX-compiled snippets, figures (using comprev-figure-construction skill), and supporting notebooks. Citation density ≥3.0 per paragraph; ≥35 citations per core body section; brace balance verified. Coordinator verifies: 12 body section drafts (sec 2-13) emitted; citations_per_paragraph ≥3.0 per section; ≥35 cites per core section; figure files exist and match Phase 6 verdicts.

steps.title: Blinded critics review each section across 6 tracks
steps.description: Parallel blinded critics (one critic ≠ writer per section) emit verdicts on 6 tracks: claim accuracy, citation support, figure-text alignment, scope creep, terminology consistency, novelty/balance. Each finding tagged MUST_FIX / SHOULD_CAVEAT / MINOR. Coordinator verifies: every section has a critic report; MUST_FIX items return to Phase 7 author for revision before Phase 9; revision loop bounded (max 2 rounds).

steps.title: Build BibTeX from CrossRef metadata; ASCII-only; dedupe; contamination scan
steps.description: For every cite key actually used in section drafts, fetch CrossRef metadata, emit ASCII-only BibTeX entries, dedupe across keys (DOI primary), and run contamination scan (no orphan keys, no entries without a citation in text). Coordinator verifies: |BibTeX entries| == |unique cite keys in MyST|; zero unicode in .bib; zero orphan entries; zero unresolved keys.

steps.title: 6-pass integration: transitions, cross-refs, terminology, continuity, figures, hygiene
steps.description: Pass 1 inter-section transitions; Pass 2 cross-references (sec/fig/eq); Pass 3 terminology unification per style_guide.md; Pass 4 narrative continuity (argument arc check); Pass 5 figure-text alignment; Pass 6 hygiene (whitespace, markdown lint, MyST roles). Coordinator verifies: each pass logs a diff summary; cross-ref resolver finds zero broken refs; terminology checker finds zero violations.

steps.title: Draft Section 1 (Introduction) and Section 13 (Synthesis/Reassessment) — last, citing only existing bibliography
steps.description: Write the Introduction and the Synthesis/Reassessment of Canonical VIP Disinhibitor sections last so they are evidence-bound by the body. New citations are forbidden — every cite key must already exist in the Phase 9 bibliography. Section 13 explicitly weighs evidence for/against the canonical framing using results from Sections 2–12. Coordinator verifies: Section 1 and Section 13 drafted; zero novel citations (every cite key intersects Phase 9 bib); Section 13 explicitly addresses canonical-framing reassessment.

steps.title: Blinded critic on Section 1 + Section 13 — claim/citation pair verification
steps.description: Blinded critic verifies that every novel claim in Sections 1 and 13 is supported by a citation already in Phase 9 bibliography, that the synthesis is internally consistent with body sections, and that the canonical-framing reassessment is fairly evidenced. Coordinator verifies: critic report present for both sections; MUST_FIX items return to Phase 11 author; zero novel-citation introductions in revisions.

steps.title: Render Methods.md and architecture figure from pipeline metadata
steps.description: Pull operon.frames + plan metadata + skill versions; render Methods.md (M.1 search strategy, M.2 evidence pipeline, M.3 critic protocol, M.4 figure construction, M.5 phase outcomes table, M.6 ledger snapshot) and an architecture figure showing the 20-phase pipeline. Coordinator verifies: Methods.md sections M.1–M.6 present; architecture figure file exists; every cited tool/skill traceable to a frame ID.

steps.title: Collect files, build LaTeX preamble, run hard assertions
steps.description: Collect all section MyST/LaTeX, figures, notebooks, Methods.md, bibliography; build the master LaTeX preamble; run hard assertions (REVIEW_REQUEST_CAPTURED, EVIDENCE_PARAMS_PRESENT, BIBLIOGRAPHY_TARGET_MET ≥2000, CITATIONS_PER_PARAGRAPH per section, FIGURE_VERDICTS_PRESENT, NO_PLACEHOLDERS, ZERO_ORPHAN_KEYS, MyST builds clean). Coordinator verifies: every assertion passes; build log clean; no unfilled template tokens remain (NO_PLACEHOLDERS assertion).

steps.title: Exhaustive citation-triple extraction (one triple per cite-key occurrence)
steps.description: For every cite-key occurrence in the assembled document, extract a triple (cite_key, ±10-line context window, claim_under_citation). NO sampling, NO tiering — exhaustive enumeration. Coordinator verifies: |triples| == |total cite-key occurrences in MyST|; every triple has non-empty claim text; cite_key references match Phase 9 bib.

steps.title: 5-step deep verification on every triple (parallel)
steps.description: For each triple in parallel: (1) DOI resolves; (2) title matches CrossRef; (3) authors match; (4) year/journal/volume metadata match; (5) full-text claim verification — the cited paper actually supports the claim. Emit verdicts VERIFIED / METADATA_MISMATCH / CLAIM_UNSUPPORTED / FULLTEXT_UNAVAILABLE — no tiering of importance, every triple gets all 5 steps. Coordinator verifies: every triple has a 5-step verdict; non-VERIFIED triples enumerated for Phase 17.

steps.title: Build fix requests with ±10-line context for each non-VERIFIED triple
steps.description: For each non-VERIFIED triple from Phase 16, build a structured fix_request with the failing verdict, ±10 lines of source context, and a recommended fix class (REPLACE_CITE / DROP_CITE / EDIT_CLAIM / ADD_CAVEAT). Coordinator verifies: every non-VERIFIED triple has a fix_request; fix_request schema valid; recommended fix class present.

steps.title: Execute bib and text fixes per fix_request (parallel)
steps.description: For each fix_request, produce a concrete diff: BibTeX entry change (if any) and text diff (replace_cite/drop_cite/edit_claim/add_caveat). Diffs must be non-overlapping; if two requests target overlapping lines, merge before emitting. Coordinator verifies: every fix_request has a corresponding diff; diffs non-overlapping; each diff names the originating fix_request_id.

steps.title: Apply diffs in reverse line order; rebuild bibliography; verify zero orphans
steps.description: Apply Phase 18 diffs in reverse line-order (bottom-up) to preserve line numbers; rebuild .bib from updated cite-key set; rerun orphan scan and Phase 14 hard assertions. Coordinator verifies: zero orphan cite keys; bibliography target still ≥2,000; build log clean; no triples regressed.

plan_content.name: Phase 20: Methods Ledger Refresh and Repository Push

steps.title: Re-render Methods M.5 + M.6 from final operon.frames ledger
steps.description: After all fixes are applied, re-render Methods.md sections M.5 (phase outcomes table) and M.6 (ledger snapshot) from the now-final operon.frames ledger so reproducibility metadata reflects the post-fix state. Coordinator verifies: M.5/M.6 timestamps post-Phase 19; phase outcome rows = 20; ledger snapshot frame count matches operon.frames query.

steps.title: Push all outputs to AllenNeuralDynamics/ComputationalReviewVIP via Contents API
steps.description: Push final MyST sources, figures, notebooks, bibliography, Methods.md, scope.json, provenance/, and build artifacts to https://github.com/AllenNeuralDynamics/ComputationalReviewVIP via the GitHub Contents API. Coordinator verifies: every required file pushed (manifest cross-check); commit SHA logged; CI build (GitHub Actions) green or failure surfaced as blocker.
